intro.title: Subtracting Vectors
paragraphs: Solving a subtraction problem is a two-step process:
2. Add A and έΙΤB using the same head-to-tail method from the previous lesson.
A έΙΤ B is the same as A + (έΙΤB).

segments.value: You can also subtract vectors directly by subtracting their matching components.
A έΙΤ B = έθρ2, 3έθσ έΙΤ έθρ3, 1έθσ = έθρ2 έΙΤ 3, 3 έΙΤ 1έθσ = έθρέΙΤ1, 2έθσ

questions.hint: Step 1: click Reverse B to turn B into έΙΤB = έθρέΙΤ1, έΙΤ2έθσ. Step 2: connect έΙΤB to the head of A, draw the result, and read it off έΑΦ count how far right or left and up or down it goes.
questions.explanation: A έΙΤ B becomes A + (έΙΤB). Once B is reversed to έΙΤB = έθρέΙΤ1, έΙΤ2έθσ, subtraction is the same head-to-tail process as addition: έθρ3, 1έθσ + έθρέΙΤ1, έΙΤ2έθσ = έθρ2, έΙΤ1έθσ.

questions.hint: Reverse B to get έΙΤB = έθρέΙΤ3, έΙΤ1έθσ, connect it head-to-tail to A, draw A έΙΤ B, then read it from the origin.
questions.explanation: Reverse B to έΙΤB = έθρέΙΤ3, έΙΤ1έθσ, then add: έθρ2, 3έθσ + έθρέΙΤ3, έΙΤ1έθσ = έθρέΙΤ1, 2έθσ. Subtracting is just adding the opposite.

questions.hint: Reverse B to get έΙΤB = έθρέΙΤ1, έΙΤ3έθσ, then add it to A head-to-tail.
questions.explanation: A έΙΤ B = A + (έΙΤB) = έθρέΙΤ2, 1έθσ + έθρέΙΤ1, έΙΤ3έθσ = έθρέΙΤ3, έΙΤ2έθσ.

questions.hint: Reverse B to get έΙΤB = έθρ1, έΙΤ1έθσ, then add it to A head-to-tail.
questions.explanation: A έΙΤ B = A + (έΙΤB) = έθρ2, έΙΤ2έθσ + έθρ1, έΙΤ1έθσ = έθρ3, έΙΤ3έθσ.

questions.hint: Reverse B to get έΙΤB = έθρ1, έΙΤ2έθσ, then add it to A head-to-tail. Watch the signs carefully.
questions.explanation: A έΙΤ B = A + (έΙΤB) = έθρ3, έΙΤ2έθσ + έθρ1, έΙΤ2έθσ = έθρ4, έΙΤ4έθσ.
